entry_title*: Test data: Mesopelagic organisms measured with a towed body deployed from RV Kronprins Haakon
summary: Broad band backscatter from organisms in the mesopelagic zone (200-1000 m depth) measured with the towed vehicle MESSOR operating 38 kHz in CW and 70, 120, 200, and 333 in FM mode.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: A layer of Atlantic herring (Clupea harengus) measured with keel-mounted echo sounders on RV G.O. Sars
summary: A layer of herring was measured with keel-mounted echo sounders from the research vessel G.O. Sars with 18 kHz in CW and 38, 70, 120, 200 and 333 kHz in FM.  Average length of the herring was 30 cm.
calibration note: Bad calibration with WC57.2 for all
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Atlantic herring (Clupea harengus) measured with an acoustic probe deployed from RV G.O. Sars
summary: Individual herring measured with a probe with 38 kHz in CW and 70, 120 and 200 kHz in FM mode, lowered to 100 m depth. Average length of the herring was 30 cm.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Lesser sandeel (Ammodytes marinus) measured with narrow-beam 200 kHz transducer deployed from MS Eros
summary: A school of sandeel measured with a narrowbeam 200 kHz transducer in FM mode lowered to 15 m depth from a rope suspended from a crane on the ship. Some noise from the ships instruments.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Northern krill (Meganyctiphanes norvegica) measured with a probe deployed from RV G.O. Sars
summary: Individually resolved krill measured with a probe with 38 kHz in CW, and 70, 120 and 200 kHz in FM mode, at 50 m depth.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: 500 Atlantic salmon (Salmo salar) in a net pen with restricted surface access measured with upwards facing echo sounders
summary: Salmon in a netpen masured with 70, 120 and 200 kHz echo sounders placed under the net pen in FM mode, facing upwards. The salmon were deprived of surface access, decreasing amount of air in the swim bladder with time.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Mesopelagic organisms measured with a towed body deployed from RV G.O. Sars
summary: Broad band backscatter from organisms in the mesopelagic zone (200-1000 m depth) measured with the towed vehicle MESSOR with 38 kHz in CW and 70, 120, 200, 333 in FM mode.  Individual scatterers can be resolved.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Atlantic mackerel (Scomber scombrus) measured with keel-mounted echo sounders on MS Libas
summary: Mackel was measured with keel-mounted echo sounders from the fishing vessel MS Libas during fisheries. 18 kHz in CW mode and 38, 70, 120, 200 and 333 kHz in FM mode.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Bluefin tuna (Thunnus thynnus) measured with keel-mounted echo sounders on MS M. Ytterstad
summary: Bluefin tuna measured with 18 and 38 kHz in CW, and 70, 120 and 200 kHz in FM mode. 70 and 120 kHz Spike noise from sonars included in the data .
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Haddock (Melanogrammus aeglefinus) and whiting (Merlangius merlangus) measured with keel- mounted echo sounders on MS Kings Bay
summary: Haddock and whiting measured with 18 and 38 kHz in CW and 70, 120 and 200 kHz in FM mode during research survey.
calibration note: FM calibration with 2 spheres for 200 kHz, one for the rest
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Zooplankton measured with keel-mounted echo sounders on RV Kristine Bonnevie
summary: Layers of different zooplankton were identified by multinet sampling showing layers dominated by copepods and echinoderm larvae, respectively. The echosounders were operated in CW and FM mode for the 18 kHz channel, and for the 38, 120, and 200 kHz channels, respectively.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Pelagic fish and northern krill containing layers measured with keel-mounted echo sounders on RV G.O. Sars
summary: Layers of pelagic fish and krill measured with 18 and 38 kHz in CW mode and 70, 120, 200 and 333 kHz in FM mode measured during installation of Hywind Tampen wind farm in the North Sea
species: Muellers pearlside mackerel herring krill blue whiting
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Atlantic salmon (Salmo salar) in a net pen measured with upwards facing echo sounders
summary: Salmon  measured with echo sounders at 25 m depth facing upwards inside a netpen. 38, 70, 120, 200 and 333 kHz were operated in FM mode. The average fork length of the salmon was 415 mm and average weight was 884 g. Four different pulse type settings were used sequentially.
calibration note: Several FM settings - special calibration file
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway
Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Capelin (Mallotus villosus) measured with keel-mounted echo sounders on RV Johan Hjort
summary: Capelin measured with keel mounted echo sounders with 18 and 38 kHz in FM mode, and 120, 200 and 333 kHz in CW mode.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Atlantic cod (Gadus morhua) measured with keel- mounted echo sounders on  RV Johan Hjort
summary: Cod close to bottom measured with keel mounted echo sounders with 18 and 38 kHz in FM mode, and 120, 200 and 333 kHz in CW mode. This dataset demonstrates challenges with the blind zone for bottom fish.
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway

entry_title*: Test data: Calibration sphere measured with keel-mounted echo sounders on RV G.O. Sars
summary: Tungsten carbide sphere with 38.1 mm diameter (WC38.1) at 28 m depth was measured with keel mounted echo sounders from RV G.O. Sars. 18, 38,70,120, 200 and 333 kHz were using sequential pinging with every second ping in CW and FM mode, and all transducers transmitting simultaneously.
calibration note: FM calibration with 1-2 spheres, 18 kHz FM failed
datacenter*: Institute of Marine Research, Norwegian Marine Data Centre, Norway
